summary: Descriptive overview of easements in substantive law: appurtenant vs in gross, creation, termination.
key_points: appurtenant vs in gross is a fundamental concept in property law.
Legal standards for easements are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine easements in litigation.
The doctrine of easements has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding easements requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
sources: Supreme Court opinions (Kelo v. New London)
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
